tasks.description: FEAT-035 [2/2] Improvement ideas UI — 'Add Improvement' dialog on detail panels, improvements page, sidebar count badge
acceptance_criteria: Add Improvement button on step-detail-panel, section-detail-panel, and touchpoint-detail-panel — opens Radix Dialog with title (required), description (textarea), priority dropdown (low/medium/high/critical, default medium). Pre-fills linked entity ID (linked_step_id, linked_section_id, or linked_touchpoint_id respectively). Calls createImprovementIdea() from client.ts. Toast on success/error.
New page at /w/[workspaceId]/improvements — server component page.tsx fetches improvement ideas, passes to client improvements-view.tsx

Improvements view: filterable list by status tabs (All, Proposed, Approved, In Progress, Completed, Rejected) and priority filter dropdown. Each card shows: title, status badge (colored by status), priority badge, linked element name (clickable link to canvas tab), description preview (truncated). Cards in a grid or list layout matching dark theme.
Inline status change via dropdown on each card — calls updateImprovementIdea()

Sidebar nav item 'Improvements' with Lightbulb icon from lucide-react, positioned between Prioritization and Comments. Badge showing open count (status NOT IN completed, rejected). Badge uses fetch in useEffect on mount.
Add 'improvements' to workspace-shell.tsx reserved paths array (line 47)
Add !p.includes('/improvements') to sidebar.tsx Workflows active check (line 39)

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/comments/page.tsx — server component pattern (fetch + pass to view)
src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/comments/comments-view.tsx — filterable list view with category tabs pattern
src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/activity/activity-view.tsx — list view with filter tabs + Load More pattern
patterns_to_follow: Server page.tsx: createServerSupabaseClient() → fetch improvement_ideas → pass to client view component
Client view: filter tabs for status, map cards, inline status dropdown, linked entity clickable links

Detail panel button: follow existing button patterns in step-detail-panel. Use Radix Dialog (Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter) from @radix-ui/react-dialog (import DialogPrimitive for Title to avoid BUG-023 pattern)
Sidebar badge: useState + useEffect to fetch open count. Badge styled like status badges (text-[10px] px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent-blue)] text-white)
Status badges: proposed=gray, approved=blue, in_progress=yellow, completed=green, rejected=red — match existing step status badge colors
Priority badges: low=gray, medium=blue, high=yellow, critical=red
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — fetchImprovementIdeas returns ImprovementIdea[] directly
workspace-shell reserved paths array — must add 'improvements' or page shows empty canvas
Sidebar Workflows active check — must exclude /improvements or nav item doesn't highlight correctly
React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null)
Custom DialogTitle vs DialogPrimitive.Title — use DialogPrimitive.Title to avoid a11y warning (BUG-023 pattern)

tasks.special_instructions: Client wrappers already exist in client.ts: fetchImprovementIdeas, createImprovementIdea, updateImprovementIdea, deleteImprovementIdea. Types exist in database.ts: ImprovementIdea, ImprovementStatus, ImprovementPriority. API routes already exist at /api/v1/improvement-ideas. This is PURELY UI work — no API or type changes needed. For the page.tsx server component, fetch improvement_ideas from Supabase with select('*') ordered by created_at desc. For linked entity display, the improvement idea has linked_step_id/linked_section_id/linked_touchpoint_id — fetch the entity name from a join or secondary query for display. Simplest: store entity_name in a column, or fetch entities separately. Since the table doesn't have entity_name, the page.tsx should join or the view should resolve names client-side by fetching steps/sections/touchpoints for the workspace.

tasks.description: Delete Perspective button label should say 'Delete Perspective' for specificity
acceptance_criteria: Delete Perspective confirmation dialog footer button reads 'Delete Perspective' instead of 'Delete'
Matches specificity pattern of other dialogs (e.g., workspace delete says 'Delete' but context is clear from title)

patterns_to_follow: Find the Delete Perspective Radix Dialog footer button (added in BUG-025 iter 107), change label from 'Delete' to 'Delete Perspective'

tasks.special_instructions: Trivial label change. The Delete Perspective dialog was added in iteration 107 (BUG-025). Find the destructive Button in the DialogFooter for perspective deletion and change its text from 'Delete' to 'Delete Perspective'.

tasks.description: Prioritization empty state CTA — add link to navigate to first workflow canvas
acceptance_criteria: When no items have effort/impact scores, the empty state shows a 'Go to Canvas' link below the existing message
Link navigates to /w/{workspaceId}/{firstTabId} — uses tabs from props or context. If no tabs exist, link is hidden.

patterns_to_follow: PrioritizationView receives tabs data (check props/context). Use first workflow tab for link target.
Follow existing empty state patterns in the codebase — guidance text + action link

tasks.special_instructions: The prioritization-view.tsx already has an empty state message. Add a Link component below it pointing to the first tab. The view likely receives tabs from the page.tsx server component or useWorkspace() context. Check how the component gets tab data before adding the link.

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed. Types and client wrappers were built in FEAT-035 [1/2].

testing_plan.regression_reason: Risk score 7 from iter 107 (schema change + >5 files + shared components). Iter 107 ran regression but scoring requires it here too.
